findings.title: Possible WAF bypass: SQL-injection reached origin
findings.description: HTTP request containing SQL-injection payload in the query string was answered 200 OK by origin. Example URI: /search?q=1%27%20OR%201%3D1--.
findings.recommendation: Enable AWSManagedRulesSQLiRuleSet on the affected ACL.
findings.impact: Attack-shaped traffic is reaching your origin uninspected. If the payload is genuinely malicious, your WAF is providing no defense against this signature class. Direct relevance to SOC 2 CC6.6, PCI-DSS 6.6, ISO 27001 A.13.1.2.
suggested_actions: Enable a managed rule group that covers this signature class. For shellshock / CVE patterns: AWSManagedRulesUnixRuleSet or AWSManagedRulesKnownBadInputsRuleSet. For SQLi gaps: AWSManagedRulesSQLiRuleSet. For XSS / Common: AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet.
Deploy the chosen group in COUNT mode first; review the false-positive sample over 7 days; promote to BLOCK only after vetting.
findings.verify_by: Replay a captured suspicious request from the audit's `suspicious_request_sample` against the WAF after rollout — it should return 403.
findings.disclaimer: RuleIQ does not generate WAF rules. Recommendations point to AWS-maintained managed groups and high-level configuration changes. Deploy new rules in COUNT mode for 7+ days before promoting to BLOCK to assess false-positive risk. Test in non-production WAFs when possible. Rollback plan: detach the new rule group via the WAF console.

findings.title: Dead rule: BlockLegacyAdminPath
findings.description: Rule 'BlockLegacyAdminPath' matched zero requests in the last 30 days.
findings.recommendation: Verify with the original author whether this protection is still required.
findings.impact: If this rule was intended to be active, the traffic it was supposed to block is no longer being inspected. If obsolete, it's creating noise that slows incident response and inflates your rule-count quota.
suggested_actions: Review with the original author / team. If the rule's purpose is no longer relevant, delete it.
If the purpose is current but the rule isn't matching, audit the rule's statement against current traffic patterns — usually a field-name or encoding mismatch.
findings.verify_by: Confirm the rule's hit count over the next 30 days remains zero; OR if you intend it to fire, generate synthetic test traffic matching its statement and verify a hit.
findings.disclaimer: RuleIQ does not generate WAF rules. Recommendations point to AWS-maintained managed groups and high-level configuration changes. Deploy new rules in COUNT mode for 7+ days before promoting to BLOCK to assess false-positive risk. Test in non-production WAFs when possible. Rollback plan: detach the new rule group via the WAF console.

findings.title: Contradicting rules on /admin
findings.description: Rule 'AllowOfficeIPRange' permits the office IP range while 'BlockOfficeIPRangeOnAdmin' blocks the same range on /admin. Rule priority will silently determine the outcome.
findings.recommendation: Confirm desired behaviour with the security owner and consolidate into a single explicit rule with the correct priority.
findings.impact: Two rules with overlapping or identical match conditions create unpredictable evaluation order, and one is effectively dead code. Cleanup reduces noise in your audit and speeds future incident response.
suggested_actions: Consolidate into one ACL on the resource. Conflicting priorities can produce unpredictable behavior.
Verify rule statements are identical before merging — subtle differences (text transformations, field-to-match) are why duplicates were created in the first place.
findings.verify_by: After consolidation: re-run a RuleIQ audit and confirm no duplicate finding is produced for this rule pair.
findings.disclaimer: RuleIQ does not generate WAF rules. Recommendations point to AWS-maintained managed groups and high-level configuration changes. Deploy new rules in COUNT mode for 7+ days before promoting to BLOCK to assess false-positive risk. Test in non-production WAFs when possible. Rollback plan: detach the new rule group via the WAF console.

findings.title: Duplicate rule: BlockMaliciousIPsDuplicate
findings.description: Rule 'BlockMaliciousIPsDuplicate' references the same IP set as 'BlockKnownMaliciousIPs' with no additional scope.
findings.recommendation: Delete BlockMaliciousIPsDuplicate after confirming hit counts.
findings.impact: Low-risk rule cleanup. Reduces console clutter, improves onboarding for new engineers, and shrinks your security review surface area.
suggested_actions: Verify with the rule's original author whether the protection is still needed. If obsolete, delete the rule to reduce console clutter and audit surface area.
findings.verify_by: After deletion: confirm the rule is removed via `aws wafv2 get-web-acl` and that the next 30-day audit no longer flags it.
findings.disclaimer: RuleIQ does not generate WAF rules. Recommendations point to AWS-maintained managed groups and high-level configuration changes. Deploy new rules in COUNT mode for 7+ days before promoting to BLOCK to assess false-positive risk. Test in non-production WAFs when possible. Rollback plan: detach the new rule group via the WAF console.

findings.title: FMS-managed rule: FMS-AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet
findings.description: Controlled by your central security admin via Firewall Manager.
findings.recommendation: Escalate to the central security team or accept as out-of-scope.
findings.impact: You cannot fix this directly — the rule is controlled by another team via Firewall Manager. But it appears in your audit report and must either be escalated or formally accepted as out-of-scope.
suggested_actions: Flag to the central security / Firewall Manager admin team. The rule is controlled by a delegated admin account and cannot be modified locally.
findings.verify_by: The FMS admin team confirms either intent or remediation for the central policy.
findings.disclaimer: RuleIQ does not generate WAF rules. Recommendations point to AWS-maintained managed groups and high-level configuration changes. Deploy new rules in COUNT mode for 7+ days before promoting to BLOCK to assess false-positive risk. Test in non-production WAFs when possible. Rollback plan: detach the new rule group via the WAF console.
